page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data shows strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and large-block memory read/write bandwidths placing in the top 25%. CPU performance is also strong in extended instruction tests and single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core processing, database operations, and static web serving fall into the average tier. Notably, LLM text generation speeds for both small and medium models are weak, placing in the bottom 25%, though prompt processing speeds remain average.

The instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high single-core compute speeds and high-speed networking without the need for local NVMe storage. The baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps makes it highly efficient for network-bound applications. However, the lack of local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, making it unsuitable for heavy local database caching or GPU-accelerated machine learning. It is best suited for high-throughput network applications, CPU-heavy general-purpose workloads, and memory-intensive processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 8252C processor running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on its 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage and communication. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory scores and strong large-block read/write bandwidths, alongside strong CPU extended instruction capabilities. Conversely, LLM text generation speeds are weak, and general database operations perform in the average tier. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for network-bound and high-frequency compute workloads, though the absence of local NVMe drives is a notable tradeoff. It is best suited for high-throughput network applications, general-purpose enterprise workloads, and CPU-intensive processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 48 vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-frequency compute and network-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a 48-vCPU general-purpose server with 192 GB RAM, 100 Gbps networking, and a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-frequency, network-optimized general purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier benchmarked memory performance
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
No local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage
Suited for network-bound applications, high-frequency compute, and general-purpose workloads
